source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/06_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/독일 BfarM 등록/D. 기술문서 최신본/6. Product Verification and Validation/1. Test report/[HnX-P1, PB] IEC 60601-1-2 Test report (ed.4.1, Kolas).pdf

document.title: TEST REPORT - Report No. KES-EM243955-R2
authorityOrJurisdiction: Korea Laboratory Accreditation Scheme (KOLAS), Republic of KOREA

sourceSummary.oneLine: KOLAS-accredited KES EMC test report KES-EM243955-R2 for H&abyz Portable X-Ray System HnX-P1 and variants INNOVISION-P5 and HnX-PB.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports product verification and validation by documenting EMC emissions and immunity testing against IEC/EN 60601-1-2 and related standards.
keyPoints: Test item is a Portable X-Ray System with model name HnX-P1 and variant models INNOVISION-P5 and HnX-PB.
Date of test was Nov. 21, 2024 to Nov. 28, 2024.
Report revision history shows KES-EM243955-R2 issued on May. 20, 2025 as a reissuance due to the addition of a derivative.
The report states that the test results are under the scope accredited by KOLAS, which signed the ILAC-MRA.
Summary of test results reports Pass for listed emission and immunity test items.

concepts.name: Basic safety and essential performance
concepts.evidence: Description of Basic Safety and Essential Performance

concepts.name: KOLAS-accredited test report
concepts.evidence: The above test report provides test result(s) under the scope accredited by Korea Laboratory Accreditation Scheme (KOLAS), which signed the ILAC-MRA.

evidence.claim: The report number and revision is KES-EM243955-R2.
The tested product is a Portable X-Ray System model HnX-P1.
evidence.evidenceText: Test item : Portable X-Ray System; Model name : HnX-P1

evidence.claim: The report identifies INNOVISION-P5 and HnX-PB as variant models.
HnX-PB differs from HnX-P1 only by appearance design, with interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, and software the same.
evidence.evidenceText: The variant model(HnX-PB) is only changed the appearance design compared to the existing model HnX-P1, and the interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, etc. are all the same.

evidence.claim: INNOVISION-P5 is a model name used by DK medical systems as legal manufacturer.
evidence.evidenceText: INNOVISION-P5 is a model name used by DK medical systems, a Legal manufacturer.

evidence.claim: The test period was Nov. 21, 2024 through Nov. 28, 2024.
The R2 report was reissued on May. 20, 2025 due to the addition of a derivative.
evidence.evidenceText: May. 20, 2025 KES-EM243955-R2 Reissuance due to the addition of a derivative

evidence.claim: The report states KOLAS accreditation and ILAC-MRA status for the reported test results.
evidence.evidenceText: The above test report provides test result(s) under the scope accredited by Korea Laboratory Accreditation Scheme (KOLAS), which signed the ILAC-MRA.

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 is intended for qualified or trained physicians or technicians to acquire X-ray images of patient anatomy and is not intended for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 portable X-ray System is a medical device intended for use by a qualified/trained physician or technician for the purpose of acquiring X-ray images of the desired parts of patient’s anatomy ... This system is not intended for mammography.

evidence.claim: Basic safety and essential performance criteria include X-ray output within ±10% of set value and proper Bluetooth functionality.
evidence.evidenceText: X-ray output must be within ±10% of the set value ... Bluetooth functionality must operate without issues and function properly.

evidence.claim: The emissions tests were performed to CISPR 11 and EN 55011 Group 1, Class A, among other standards.
evidence.evidenceText: The emissions tests were performed according to following regulations: CISPR 11:2015+A1:2016+A2:2019 Group 1, Class A ... EN 55011:2016/A2:2021 Group 1, Class A

evidence.claim: Conducted emissions, radiated emissions, harmonic current emissions, and voltage fluctuations and flicker emissions passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Conducted Emissions Pass ... Radiated Emissions Pass ... Harmonics Current Emissions Pass ... Voltage changes, voltage fluctuations and Flicker Emissions Pass

evidence.claim: Immunity tests listed in the summary passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Electrostatic Discharge Pass ... Radiated RF electromagnetic Field Pass ... Electrical Fast Transients/Burst Pass ... Surge Pass ... Conducted RF Disturbance Pass ... Power Frequency Magnetic Field Pass ... Voltage Dips, Short Interruptions and voltage variations Pass

evidence.claim: Telecommunication port conducted emissions were not applicable because no IP is assigned.
evidence.evidenceText: Conducted Emissions at Telecommunication Ports ... Test Date N/A ... Remarks Does not apply because no IP is assigned.

evidence.claim: During multiple immunity tests, no degradation of performance was found.
evidence.evidenceText: Remarks During the test, there are no degradation of performance was found.

evidence.claim: The test plan identifies emissions as Class A Group 1 and immunity as professional healthcare facility environment.
evidence.evidenceText: EMISSION: Class A / Group 1; IMMUNITY: Professional healthcare facility environment

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is MFDS/domestic, but the source text identifies KES Co., Ltd. and KOLAS accreditation and does not mention MFDS.
This source is an EMC test report and does not itself state regulatory approval, registration, or market authorization status.
Some OCR text includes corrupted symbols or unit characters, but the main regulatory and test-result facts are readable.
Conducted disturbance reference standard appears as IEC 61000-4-6:2023 in the main test section and IEC 61000-4-6:2013 in Annex 1, creating conflicting evidence.
